pi_items.source: Analyzed seed_bahmni.py line 29-52 (PATIENT_DATA list) and line 269 (identifier formula). Lisa Thompson is at index 7 in PATIENT_DATA (8th patient). Using enumerate(PATIENT_DATA, 1), she gets i=8, resulting in identifier f'BAH{8+1:06d}' = BAH000009. Cross-verified with medication_allergy_reconciliation task.json which correctly assigns BAH000008 to Aisha Abdullahi (index 6, i=7).
seed_bahmni.py line 38: ('Lisa', 'Thompson', 'F', 1960) in PATIENT_DATA array at index 7
setup_bahmni.sh line 14: OPENMRS_ADMIN_USERNAME='superman'; seed_bahmni.py line 226: default='superman'
setup_bahmni.sh line 15: OPENMRS_ADMIN_PASSWORD='Admin123'; seed_bahmni.py line 228: default='Admin123'

privileged_info_summary: Patient name 'Lisa Thompson' is correct. The administrative credentials (superman / Admin123) are verified across multiple configuration files. However, the patient identifier has an error: the metadata claims BAH000008, but based on the seed_bahmni.py patient enumeration logic, Lisa Thompson should have identifier BAH000009. Aisha Abdullahi correctly has BAH000008. Patient data is synthetic, generated programmatically using US Census Bureau public names data with randomly assigned birth years. The task requires voiding one of two duplicate 'Vitals' encounters in the Bahmni/OpenMRS system.
